metadata.name: typesystem
metadata.summary: A type system library for Python

functions.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokenize_yaml.validate_yaml
documentation:
Parse and validate a YAML string, returning positionally marked error
messages on parse or validation failures.

content - A YAML string or bytestring.
validator - A Field instance or Schema class to validate against.

Returns a two-tuple of (value, error_messages)

variables.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokenize_yaml.yaml
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokenize_yaml.SafeLoader
documentation: type: ignore

methods.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokens.Token.lookup
documentation: Given an index, lookup a child token within this structure.

methods.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokens.Token.lookup_key
documentation: Given an index, lookup a token for a dictionary key within this structure.

functions.name: typesystem.tokenize.tokenize_json.validate_json
documentation:
Parse and validate a JSON string, returning positionally marked error
messages on parse or validation failures.

content - A JSON string or bytestring.
validator - A Field instance or Schema class to validate against.

Returns a two-tuple of (value, error_messages)

variables.name: typesystem.forms.jinja2
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: typesystem.base.Message

methods.name: typesystem.base.Message.__init__
documentation:
text - The error message. 'May not have more than 100 characters'
code - An optional error code, eg. 'max_length'
key - An optional key of the message within a single parent. eg. 'username'
index - The index of the message
    within a nested object. eg. ['users', 3, 'username']

Optionally either:

position - The start and end position of the error message
    within the raw content.

Or:

start_position - The start position of the error message within the raw content.
end_position - The end position of the error message within the raw content.
An individual error message, within a ValidationError.

classes.name: typesystem.base.BaseError

methods.name: typesystem.base.BaseError.__init__
documentation:
Either instantiated with a single message, like so:

text - The error message. 'May not have more than 100 characters'
code - An optional error code, eg. 'max_length'
key - An optional key of the message within a single parent. eg. 'username'

Or instantiated with a list of error messages:

messages - A list of all the messages in the error.

methods.name: typesystem.base.BaseError.messages
documentation:
Return a list of all the messages.

add_prefix - An optional key to add to the index of all returned messages.
             Useful in nested objects when validation needs to accumulate
             all the child messages for each item in the parent object.

A validation or parse error, containing one or more error messages.
Error information is accessible either by accessing as a dict-like object,
eg. `dict(error)` or by returning the list of messages with `error.messages()`.

ValidationError is either raised, in the `validate()` usage:

value = MySchema.validate(data)

Or returned in the `validate_or_error()` usage:

value, error = MySchema.validate_or_error(data)

classes.name: typesystem.base.ParseError
documentation: Raised by `typesystem.tokenize_json()` and `typesystem.tokenize_yaml()`.

classes.name: typesystem.base.ValidationError
documentation: Raised by `.validate()` or returned by `.validate_or_error()`.

classes.name: typesystem.base.ValidationResult

methods.name: typesystem.base.ValidationResult.__init__
documentation:
Either:

value - The validated data.

Or:

error - The validation error.

A pair providing the validated data or validation error.
Typically unpacked like so:

value, error = MySchema.validate_or_error(data)

modules.name: typesystem.composites
documentation: The following fields are required for complete JSON schema support,
but are undocumented as we don't recommend using them directly.

classes.name: typesystem.composites.NeverMatch
documentation: Doesn't ever match.

classes.name: typesystem.composites.OneOf
documentation:
Must match exactly one of the sub-items.

You'll almost always want to just use `Union` instead of this, which is an
"anyOf" test.

classes.name: typesystem.composites.AllOf
documentation:
Must match all of the sub-items.

You should instead consolidate into a single type, or use
schema inheritence instead of this.

classes.name: typesystem.composites.Not
documentation:
Must match all of the sub-items.

You should use custom validation instead.

classes.name: typesystem.composites.IfThenElse
documentation:
Conditional sub-item matching.

You should use custom validation instead.

classes.name: typesystem.unique.Uniqueness

methods.name: typesystem.unique.Uniqueness.make_hashable
documentation: Coerce a primitive into a uniquely hashable type, for uniqueness checks.

A set-like class that tests for uniqueness of primitive types.
Ensures the `True` and `False` are treated as distinct from `1` and `0`,
and coerces non-hashable instances that cannot be added to sets,
into hashable representations that can.

classes.name: typesystem.fields.Any
documentation: Always matches.

classes.name: typesystem.fields.Const
documentation: Only ever matches the given given value.

modules.name: typesystem.json_schema
documentation: Provides 'typesystem.from_json_schema()' and 'typesystem.to_json_schema()'.

functions.name: typesystem.json_schema.type_from_json_schema
documentation: Build a typed field or union of typed fields from a JSON schema object.

functions.name: typesystem.json_schema.get_valid_types
documentation: Returns a two-tuple of `(type_strings, allow_null)`.

functions.name: typesystem.json_schema.from_json_schema_type
documentation: Build a typed field from a JSON schema object.
